food.name: Salmon

identity.basisReason: Raw per-100g edible-flesh basis keeps the entry aligned with a base whole-food ingredient rather than a cooked preparation.

scoreReadiness.summary: Strong enough for launch scripting as a raw salmon baseline, but a few protein-quality style fields are still legacy calibration proxies and salmon source variation can move the fat profile.
blockers: collagen and protein-quality proxy metrics still need either exact-source backing or deliberate removal
wild-versus-farmed and species variation can shift the exact fat and omega-3 profile meaningfully

sourceNotes: Identity locked to raw salmon flesh, using Atlantic salmon style per-100 g baseline values.
Core nutrient values align with USDA FoodData Central style raw Atlantic salmon entries.
Raw basis kept intentionally, not cooked salmon values.

topPros.title: easy to build a strong meal around
topPros.explanation: It works as a genuine centrepiece instead of needing lots of nutritional excuses.

topPros.title: very satisfying as a protein source
has unusually strong trust with viewers
topPros.explanation: People already read it as a serious healthy-food choice, which matches its overall case.

topCons.title: quality varies with sourcing
topCons.explanation: Wild vs farmed differences matter in practice.

topCons.title: needs good storage and handling
topCons.explanation: A practical downside compared with some shelf-stable foods.

explanation.summary: Vitamins are carrying this food most, while minerals are holding it back most. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 56.8. Section scores: fats 80, proteins 60, vitamins 84, minerals 0, pros 66.7, cons 50.

closing.summary: Salmon has a genuinely strong overall case. The practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.

scenes.narrationText: 13g of fat. Omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. For meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
scenes.subtitleText: 13g of fat. Omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. For meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.

scenes.narrationText: 0g of carbs. The rest is not doing much. In this category, carbs barely matter.
scenes.subtitleText: 0g of carbs. The rest is not doing much. In this category, carbs barely matter.

scenes.narrationText: 20.4g of protein. Protein quantity is doing most of the work. Strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
scenes.subtitleText: 20.4g of protein. Protein quantity is doing most of the work. Strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.

scenes.narrationText: Vitamin b12 at 117% daily value, vitamin d at 66% daily value stand out most. That is major vitamin support.
scenes.subtitleText: Vitamin b12 at 117% daily value, vitamin d at 66% daily value stand out most. That is major vitamin support.

scenes.narrationText: Potassium at 8% daily value stands out most. Useful, but still pretty limited.
scenes.subtitleText: Potassium at 8% daily value stands out most. Useful, but still pretty limited.

scenes.narrationText: On the plus side: easy to build a strong meal around. It works as a genuine centrepiece instead of needing lots of nutritional excuses. Very satisfying as a protein source. That makes it practical in real meals. Has unusually strong trust with viewers. People already read it as a serious healthy-food choice, which matches its overall case.
